creator: gnats@duck.com
text:
So, I spun up a couple (OK, four) bhyve vms to test what behaviors previous versions exhibited. These tests seem to indicate that we've had this behavior from the start.

FreeBSD luna-vm 13.2-RELEASE FreeBSD 13.2-RELEASE releng/13.2-n254617-525ecfdad597 GENERIC amd64
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
good recv: 588

FreeBSD luna-vm 13.2-RELEASE-p11 FreeBSD 13.2-RELEASE-p11 GENERIC amd64
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
good recv: 588

FreeBSD luna-vm 13.3-RELEASE FreeBSD 13.3-RELEASE releng/13.3-n257428-80d2b634ddf0 GENERIC amd64
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
good recv: 58

FreeBSD luna-vm 14.0-RELEASE FreeBSD 14.0-RELEASE #0 releng/14.0-n265380-f9716eee8ab4: Fri Nov 10 05:57:23 UTC 2023     root@releng1.nyi.freebsd.org:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/GENERIC amd64
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
good recv: 688

just a little footnote, but the first run around with 13.2-RELEASE (no patch level) I was able to get it to do this (sorted itself after a reboot, however.):
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1

bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
good recv: 588
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
good recv: 588
root@luna-vm:~ # ./a.out
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1

bad recv: -1

bad recv: -1

bad recv: -1
bad recv: -1

bad recv: -1

good recv: 588
